reminder.notes:
New app icon and alarm screen colors follow the app theme

### Features

- New app icon: a checklist notepad with a green checkmark on a green gradient, replacing the
  previous calendar icon on blue. Matches the app's own accent color.
- The alarm ring screen's Dismiss button and loading-state color now use the app's green theme
  accent instead of a hardcoded red.

### Bug fixes

No user-facing bug fixes in this release.

ai-scanner.notes:
# AI Scanner 1.1.0 — screenshots are now understood, not just OCR'd

This release replaces the old text-only summarizer with an on-device vision model that actually looks at each screenshot, so the notes describe what a screenshot is and the one useful thing to remember from it — instead of dumping the raw OCR text back at you.

### Features
- Vision summaries: a new on-device model (Gemma 4 E2B) reads the screenshot itself — layout, app, icons, images — and writes a short, useful note. OCR is now used only to keep exact names, prices, and codes correct.
- Better search: because summaries are meaningful now, semantic search over them is much more relevant.
- Opportunistic inbuilt Gemini Nano: on supported flagship devices, an on-device image caption is folded in as an extra hint. Nothing changes on other devices.
- Fully offline and zero-cost as always: the vision model downloads once (about 2.7 GB, best on phones with 8 GB+ RAM) and everything runs on-device. No account, no cloud, no uploads.

### Bug fixes
- Fixed the core problem where summaries just echoed the OCR text and were not useful. The pipeline no longer feeds flattened OCR to a tiny text model; it looks at the actual screenshot.
- Removed a truncation that cut long screenshots down to the first 1000 characters before summarizing.
- The old Gemma 3 1B model is automatically removed once the new model is in place, reclaiming its storage.
- Devices that cannot run the vision model degrade cleanly to a text summary instead of failing.

mirrordrive.notes:
# MirrorDrive 0.13.2

### Features
- No new features. This release fixes the in-app updater and one playback behavior.

### Bug fixes
- The in-app update check now actually works on a real phone. It previously gave up after 8 seconds while the network request itself could take up to 20, so on a normal mobile connection the check was abandoned before it finished and the update prompt never appeared. The check is now non-blocking: the app opens immediately and the update screen appears as soon as a new version is found.
- Deleting a file that is currently playing now stops playback at the same time, instead of continuing to play a file that no longer exists.

twinclean.notes: Diagnostic fix: the 'Connect Google Drive' failure path now shows the real underlying error instead of a generic message, so sign-in issues can actually be diagnosed. No feature changes.
